goal: Реализовать Host Bridge MVP: первый реальный Android -> Host Bridge -> local Codex runtime path без ухода в полный real thread runtime.
done: stage 1 completed: transport contract locked to http_json, ws/wss moved to explicit unsupported path, degraded runtime summary contract added, cleartext opt-in and allowlist policy fixed
stage 2 completed: Windows Host Bridge helper added with bearer auth, stdio app-server client, app/list summary path, degraded caching, and auth-first reject behavior

stage 3 completed: Android-side OkHttpHostBridgeClient and HttpJsonHostBridgeRepository integrated, runtime graph now wires the real host-backed repository, StukayAppState runs host bridge actions on a background executor with periodic probe loop and lifecycle teardown

stage 4 completed: Settings, Projects, and Diagnostics now read the shared HostBridgeConnectionState, Settings and Projects stay on summary-only runtime signal, Diagnostics keeps full telemetry detail, HostRuntimeSnapshotScope distinguishes live vs last_known snapshots, and fresh unauthorized failures no longer look cached

stage 5 completed: repo-local gates are green, helper runtime hardening closed the Windows spawn and degraded HTTP error-path bugs, Python helper tests pass in ResourceWarning-strict mode, diff-scoped transport security review found no validated security issues, the physical Pixel flow is proven over USB tether LAN, and the emulator flow is proven on medium_phone through 10.0.2.2

post-proof external review fix completed: Nearby devices posture no longer hard-blocks admissible private/local endpoints on current API 36, redirects are explicitly rejected at the Android client boundary, manual disconnect now invalidates queued probes via a thread-safe HostBridgeProbeBarrier, auth/protocol failures no longer publish stale runtime metrics as live, remote-controlled diagnostic strings are sanitized before reaching state/log/UI, helper bind host is restricted to loopback/private-only surfaces, and helper app/list inventory drains all pages before computing the runtime count
next: run final branch-wide review loop vs main
if no new findings survive validation, prepare final merge-readiness verdict
verify_status: JetBrains sync-preflight passes; targeted app regression tests for repository, redirect policy, and probe barrier are green; helper Python runtime_client and server tests are green under ResourceWarning-strict mode; :app:lintDebug, :core:model:testDebugUnitTest, :app:testDebugUnitTest, and :app:assembleDebug are green; live helper proof succeeds; physical Pixel and emulator acceptance flows are both proven
